set.body:
Problem

Gale now distinguishes ResidualConverged from ExtremeCertified, while requireConverged intentionally remains residual-based for compatibility. Scalafim graph-linalg will move partial graph spectra to the matrix-free operator path. A downstream caller can therefore retain the historical helper and accidentally accept a result that has not certified membership in the requested global spectral extreme.

Request

Add an explicit typed enforcement API, such as requireExtremeCertified on diagnostics-carrying spectral results or requireConvergence with a named acceptance policy. Preserve the existing requireConverged behavior. The failure surface must distinguish residual non-convergence from a residual-converged but uncertified spectral extreme. This is policy enforcement over the existing certificate; it must not imply that Gale can manufacture a global certificate without full-space reduction or a future validated bound.

Acceptance

- The hidden-extreme ResidualConverged regression is rejected by the strict helper even though allConverged is true.
- Dense and full-space ExtremeCertified results are accepted.
- NotConverged remains a typed convergence failure.
- The API is available on EigenDecomposition at minimum and is made consistent across other diagnostics-carrying spectral result types where appropriate.
- Existing requireConverged source and behavior compatibility is preserved.
- Scaladoc and spectral contract documentation explain residual acceptance versus strict certification.
- Shared JVM and Scala.js tests cover all three convergence states.
- A Scala 3.4 downstream consumer probe demonstrates Scalafim-compatible source consumption.
set.title: Add typed extreme-certification enforcement for downstream callers
